rationale: The cited paper examines stage-level sequential search behavior (comparison and stopping decisions), which overlaps conceptually with the citing paper’s focus on sequential consumer search and stop/continue choices under uncertainty. However, the cited study is in recommendation-assisted product search, while the citing note emphasizes belief elicitation and search-cost inference in homogeneous price search, and no direct in-text citation context is provided beyond a DOI match. Given this partial but not definitive relevance, the evidence is insufficient to safely drop.
